page_content: undergraduate/colleges-schools/education/ #collegerequirementstext) Pre-Professional/Subject Area Courses Introductory Courses: Interpreting American History: Beginnings to 1877 or HIST201 Interpreting American History: From 1865 to the Present Historical Research and Methods Seminar HIST elective - Latin America, Middle East, East Asia or Africa HIST elective- Britain and Western Erurope OR Eastern Europe and Senior Seminar Select 24 credits of History Electives 1 In addition to the required credit hours in history, the social studies education program requires 29 credit hours of course work in geography and the social sciences as outlined below: Introduction to Human Geography Select one of the following: ECON111 Thinking Like an Economist ECON175 Inequality: Determinants and Policy Remedies ECON181 Putting a Price on the Environment: An Economist's Perspective on Sustainability ECON200 Principles of Microeconomics ECON201 Principles of Macroeconomics Lower-level ECON I-series course American Government Introduction to Sociology or SOCY105 Understanding Contemporary Social Problems - Frameworks for Critical Thinking and Strategies for Solutions Pre-Professional/Education Courses Select one of the following: TLPL250 Historical and Philosophical Perspectives on Education
